name: Sumo Logic API
what_it_does: Cloud-native log analytics and SIEM platform API for running log searches, managing data sources, creating dashboards, and configuring security insights from agent workflows.
use_cases: Running log queries (Sumo Logic Query Language) from incident response agents
Querying security signals and insights for automated threat investigation
Managing log collection sources and ingest pipelines programmatically
Creating and updating dashboards and alerts via API
Exporting log data for compliance reporting and forensic analysis
not_for: Real-time streaming log ingestion (use Kafka or Kinesis)
Simple log file storage (use S3 or GCS)
best_when: Your organization uses Sumo Logic for log analytics or SIEM and you need agents to programmatically run searches, investigate security signals, or manage ingest configuration.
avoid_when: You don't use Sumo Logic or need open-source log management.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic Auth using Access ID and Access Key. No OAuth. Role-based access control manages what operations keys can perform. Keys are long-lived and rotatable.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free trial available; continuous log ingest 500MB/day free
pricing.paid_tiers: Essentials ~$3/GB/day; Enterprise pricing by ingest volume
pricing.notes: Pricing based on daily log ingest volume (GB/day). Enterprise contracts for large-scale deployments. Free tier limited to 500MB/day with 7-day retention.

performance.rate_limits: API: 240 requests/minute; Search: concurrent search limits by plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include status, id (unique error ID), errors array with code and message. Log search errors are descriptive about query syntax issues.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Search jobs are asynchronous — create job, poll for completion, fetch results. Scheduled searches are idempotent by name. Resource creation generally not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Log searches are asynchronous — must create a search job, poll status, then retrieve results (3-step process)
Sumo Logic Query Language (SPLQ) has distinct syntax from SQL or Splunk SPL
API endpoints differ by deployment region/pod — must use the correct base URL for your account
Rate limits on search API are strict — concurrent search jobs limited by plan
Access IDs and Keys are shown only once at creation — must be saved immediately
Data retention varies by plan tier — old log data may not be queryable on lower tiers

security_score_components.security_notes: Log analytics SIEM. Access ID + key auth. Logs contain sensitive operational data. RBAC for access control. SOC2 Type II certified. Data encryption at rest.
